source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW, HAD1717MCW)/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/06_Risk Management/04-01. Risk Management Report (Doc.No.A-TCF-5-037).docx

document.title: Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
standards: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EC

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for the ADD Abyz Digital Detector, Report No. A-TCF-5-037 Rev.3.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk analysis, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information for ADD.
keyPoints: ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
The device is not to be used for mammography.
Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019.
The report concludes that overall residual risks are minimized and acceptable.
No factors were identified that negatively affected the product's benefit-risk.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Contraindication for mammography
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

concepts.name: Expected service life
concepts.evidence: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After the risk control measures are applied, any residual risk shall be evaluated using the criteria defined in the risk management plan.

concepts.name: Post-production information
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall establish generic procedures to collect information from various sources

concepts.name: Benefit-risk monitoring
concepts.evidence: No factors were identified that negatively affected the product's benefit-risk.

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report number and revision are A-TCF-5-037 Rev.3.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: A-TCF-5-037 (Rev.3)

evidence.claim: The Rev.3 revision date is Nov/20/2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 3 Nov/20/2024 Revision according to PMS update

evidence.claim: The device product name is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model Name: ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is a portable digital X-ray flat panel wireless detector that can generate Images of any part of the body.

evidence.claim: The ADD system consists of a detector, power supply box, battery recharger, switch box, battery packs and a main cable.
evidence.evidenceText: This X-ray imaging system consists of an X-ray detector (an a-Si TFT sensor panel with scintillator), a power supply box, a battery recharger, switch box, battery packs and a main cable.

evidence.claim: Risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Our risk management activities follow EN ISO 14971:2019

evidence.claim: The risk analysis methods and procedures are focused on EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN 60601-1:2006/A1:2013.
evidence.evidenceText: Main methods and procedures we selected herewith are focused on EN ISO 14971:2019 and EN 60601-1:2006/A1:2013.

evidence.claim: ADD has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: The device contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: The product contains software.

evidence.claim: ADD is intended to be portable.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended to be designed portable

evidence.claim: ADD is not a life supporting device.
evidence.evidenceText: The ADD is not a life supporting device.

evidence.claim: Risk level is calculated as severity multiplied by probability.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity x Probability

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Green Zone Acceptable Region Risk Level: 1~4 Red Zone Unacceptable Region Risk Level: 5~25

evidence.claim: The report identified 30 hazards in risk evaluation, all initially unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 - 30

evidence.claim: After risk control, 30 controlled hazards were acceptable and none were unacceptable residual risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Total 30 30 - -

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are considered acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Result: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable

evidence.claim: The quality management system is based on EN ISO 13485:2016.
evidence.evidenceText: We have been operating a quality management system. Quality management system based on EN ISO13485: 2016.

evidence.claim: The second information collection period was December 15, 2022 to November 20, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 2nd Information Collection Period: December 15, 2022 ~ November 20, 2024

evidence.claim: Customer feedback for device, function, packaging, and incident was 0 for 2021 through 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Customer feedback Years Device Function Packaging Incident 2021 0 0 0 0 2022 0 0 0 0 2023 0 0 0 0 2024 0 0 0 0

evidence.claim: Incident feedback report cases to MFDS, FDA, and MHRA were 0 for 2021 through 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Incident feedback Years Report Cases MFDS FDA MHRA 2021 0 0 0 2022 0 0 0 2023 0 0 0 2024 0 0 0

evidence.claim: No factors were identified that negatively affected the product's benefit-risk.
evidence.evidenceText: No factors were identified that negatively affected the product's benefit-risk.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date evidence is conflicting: cover and Rev.3 history show Nov 20, 2024, while the conclusion signature section states Date: December 01, 2022.
Authority signal is general; the source references EU directives and incident-reporting authorities but does not identify a single issuing regulatory authority.
Source metadata productSignal lists HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the source text identifies the model name as ADD and does not state HAD1417MCW or HAD1717MCW as product models.
Some tabular evidence is flattened in preprocessing, reducing precision for totals and table relationships.
